description: LLM eval for adversarial prompt file quality (compose_adversarial output).

cases.name: developer-vague-productivity-goal

input.prior_step_summary: User is a senior backend engineer at a series-B startup. Wants to 'use AI to be more productive.' Did not specify a task, frequency, or current pain point. Mentioned Python and Postgres but not which workflows are slow.
input.next_step_topic: Step 2 skill + MCP recommendations for a backend engineer

expectations: the rendered prompt contains an adversarial framing section that tells the next-step LLM to push back
the rendered prompt explicitly references the next-step topic ('Step 2 skill + MCP recommendations for a backend engineer')
the rendered prompt preserves the prior step summary verbatim under a clearly labeled section
the framing instructs the LLM to refuse vague answers like 'use AI to be more productive'
the framing instructs the LLM to ask for evidence or concrete examples
the prompt does not invent specifics (frequency, exact pain point) that the prior summary did not state

cases.name: researcher-contradictory-signals

input.prior_step_summary: User identifies as an ML research engineer focused on interpretability. But the projects they listed are all data-pipeline ETL work in Airflow. Stated goal: 'publish a paper this year.' No mention of writing, experiments, or collaborators.
input.next_step_topic: Step 2 skill + MCP recommendations for an interpretability researcher

expectations: the framing instructs the LLM to surface contradictions when role and projects do not line up
the rendered prompt preserves the verbatim contradiction (interpretability vs ETL) in the prior step context
the rendered prompt references the next-step topic accurately
the framing tells the LLM to flag mismatch between stated identity and actual work
the prompt does not pre-resolve the contradiction or pick a side for the user

cases.name: pm-aspirational-no-anecdotes

input.prior_step_summary: User is a product manager at a mid-size SaaS. Said: 'I want to ship features faster and reduce alignment overhead with engineering.' No specific feature, no specific friction, no examples of past launches.
input.next_step_topic: Step 2 skill + MCP recommendations for a product manager

expectations: the framing instructs the LLM to refuse aspirational answers without specifics
the framing instructs the LLM to ask for the specific task and frequency
the rendered prompt preserves the user's stated goal verbatim
the rendered prompt references the next-step topic for a product manager
the framing prefers fewer concrete recommendations over many hedged ones

cases.name: nonprofit-grant-writer-realistic-goal

input.prior_step_summary: User is a development director at a small literacy nonprofit. Concrete goal: 'cut grant draft time from 3 weeks to 1 week.' Has 5 active funders, drafts roughly 2 grants per month, no engineering background, uses Google Docs.
input.next_step_topic: Step 2 skill + MCP recommendations for a nonprofit development director

expectations: the framing is preserved even when the prior summary is already concrete
the rendered prompt preserves the 3-week-to-1-week goal verbatim
the rendered prompt references the nonprofit development director topic
the framing does not condescend or assume the user is technical
the prompt does not invent funder names or grant volumes beyond what was stated
the framing leaves room to flag if no good skill/MCP fits a non-coder workflow

cases.name: content-creator-tool-fishing

input.prior_step_summary: User is a YouTube creator (350k subs, tech reviews). Asked: 'what AI tools should I use?' No stated workflow problem. Mentioned editing in DaVinci Resolve, scripts in Notion, no current bottleneck.
input.next_step_topic: Step 2 skill + MCP recommendations for a YouTube content creator

expectations: the framing instructs the LLM to refuse the open-ended 'what tools should I use' framing without a stated problem
the framing tells the LLM to ask for the current pain point or bottleneck
the rendered prompt preserves the creator context (YouTube, tech reviews) verbatim
the rendered prompt references the YouTube content creator topic
the prompt does not invent a workflow problem that the user did not mention

cases.name: exploring-empty-prior-context

input.next_step_topic: Step 1 deeper interview for an 'exploring' user

expectations: the rendered prompt handles empty prior context gracefully (no exception, no garbled output)
the rendered prompt indicates that no prior context was supplied
the rendered prompt still includes the adversarial framing block
the rendered prompt references the Step 1 deeper interview topic
the framing still tells the LLM to pull concrete facts rather than invent details

cases.name: data-scientist-weak-match-honest

input.prior_step_summary: User is a data scientist at a healthtech startup. Wants 'something to help me clean lab data faster.' Curated mapping has no skills tagged for lab data cleanup; the closest is a generic data-quality skill.
input.next_step_topic: Step 2 skill + MCP recommendations for a healthtech data scientist

expectations: the framing instructs the LLM to flag when no good option exists rather than pretending a weak match is strong
the rendered prompt preserves the lab-data-cleanup goal verbatim
the rendered prompt references the healthtech data scientist topic
the framing prefers honest 'no good fit' over a forced recommendation
the prompt does not pre-recommend the generic skill as if it were a strong match

cases.name: founder-self-described-no-evidence

input.prior_step_summary: User self-describes as 'technical founder, ex-FAANG.' Building a B2B SaaS in legal tech. Stated goal: 'build the MVP in 6 weeks.' No concrete description of the MVP, no users yet, no specific feature in flight.
input.next_step_topic: Step 2 skill + MCP recommendations for a technical founder

expectations: the framing instructs the LLM to ask for evidence (concrete examples beat self-description)
the framing tells the LLM to push for the specific MVP scope and current user signal
the rendered prompt preserves the founder self-description verbatim
the rendered prompt references the technical founder topic
the prompt does not flatter the user or treat the FAANG claim as load-bearing

cases.name: sre-strict-tolerance-on-call-burnout

input.prior_step_summary: User is a site reliability engineer at a fintech. Top problem: 'reduce on-call burnout, automate post-incident review.' Strict skill tolerance (only well-maintained tools). Uses Python and Go. 2 weeks until on-call rotation hits them again.
input.next_step_topic: Step 3 scaffold plan for an SRE building on-call tooling

expectations: the rendered prompt preserves the on-call burnout and post-incident review goals verbatim
the rendered prompt references the Step 3 scaffold plan topic for an SRE
the framing instructs the LLM to refuse vague answers and demand specifics
the framing leaves room to flag when no curated option meets strict-tolerance standards
the prompt does not invent project names that were not stated

cases.name: weekend-hacker-personal-archetype

input.prior_step_summary: User is a weekend hacker who codes for fun, not for work. Wants 'a small CLI that nags me on rest days for my running training plan.' Strict skill tolerance. Python and Rust. Time budget: ~2 hours per weekend.
input.next_step_topic: Step 3 scaffold plan for a personal-archetype weekend project

expectations: the rendered prompt preserves the rest-day-nagging goal verbatim
the rendered prompt references the Step 3 scaffold plan topic for a personal-archetype weekend project
the framing tone respects the personal/hobbyist context (no enterprise framing forced onto a weekend project)
the framing instructs the LLM to prefer fewer concrete recommendations over many hedged ones
the prompt does not invent goals beyond the rest-day nagger CLI

cases.name: consultant-multi-client-context

input.prior_step_summary: User is an independent AI consultant working across 4 client engagements: two retail (POS analytics), one biotech (lab-notebook search), one media company (content tagging). Wants 'a workflow that lets me switch context between clients without losing momentum.' No specific tool stack stated per client.
input.next_step_topic: Step 2 skill + MCP recommendations for a multi-client AI consultant

expectations: the rendered prompt preserves all four client contexts verbatim
the rendered prompt references the multi-client AI consultant topic
the framing instructs the LLM to push for specifics per client (which context-switch is most painful)
the prompt does not invent a tool stack for any of the four clients
the framing prefers concrete recommendations over many hedge-bet ones
